title: United States refund policy
text: The current United States refund policy applies to eligible purchases. A customer must request a refund within 45 calendar days of purchase. Approved refunds return to the original payment method. Final-sale items and consumed usage credits are excluded. Support must cite this version rather than an earlier policy. Requests after the window require review and are not promised.

title: Retired United States refund policy
text: This retired policy allowed refunds within 30 calendar days of purchase. It was replaced by version 3 and must not be used for current answers. The old window appears here only to test version filtering and rollback safety.

title: International returns and refunds
text: Customers outside the United States may request a return within 30 calendar days of delivery. Original shipping fees, customs duties, and import taxes are not refundable. The customer receives a return authorization before shipping an item back. Approved product charges return to the original payment method after inspection.

title: Cancel an annual subscription
text: An annual subscription can be cancelled before its renewal date to stop automatic renewal. Cancellation does not create a prorated refund. Access continues through the already-paid annual term. Workspace owners cancel from Billing, then Plans, then Turn off renewal. The confirmation page shows the final access date.

title: Cancel a monthly subscription
text: A monthly subscription can be cancelled at any time. Access continues until the next monthly billing date and no new monthly charge is created. Cancellation does not reverse a charge that has already settled. The owner uses Billing, Plans, and Cancel monthly plan.

title: Payment error E1492
text: Error code E1492 means the payment gateway timed out before returning a final authorization result. Wait 60 seconds and check the payment activity once. Do not submit the same charge repeatedly because the original authorization may still settle. If E1492 persists, collect the request identifier and contact Payment Operations.

title: Asset inventory A1492
text: Asset A1492 is a retired conference-room display assigned to the Boston office. The asset record is unrelated to checkout, charges, payment gateways, or authorization timeouts. Facilities owns disposal of this hardware item.

title: Authentication error E7710
text: Authentication error E7710 indicates that the client clock differs from trusted time by more than five minutes. Synchronize the computer with network time, restart the sign-in flow, and request a fresh challenge. Resetting the password does not correct clock skew.

title: Database error E204
text: Database error E204 reports a transaction deadlock. The client may retry the entire idempotent transaction with randomized backoff. A client must not replay only the final write. Persistent E204 events require the database team to inspect lock ordering and the transaction trace.

title: API rate limits
text: An API response with status 429 means the caller exceeded a rate limit. Read Retry-After for the minimum delay and the X-RateLimit-Reset header for the window boundary. Retry with jitter and a request budget. Increasing parallelism during throttling makes recovery slower.

title: Verify webhook signatures
text: Verify each webhook before parsing its business fields. Compute HMAC SHA-256 over the exact raw request body using the endpoint secret, compare the signature in constant time, and reject stale timestamps. JSON reserialization changes bytes and causes valid signatures to fail.

title: Configure enterprise SSO
text: An enterprise administrator begins SSO setup by verifying the company email domain with a DNS TXT record. After verification, upload identity-provider metadata, map the immutable subject identifier, test with a pilot group, and only then enforce SSO. Keep an emergency administrator outside enforcement.

title: Export billing invoices
text: Workspace owners can export invoices from Billing, then Documents. Choose a date range and select PDF or CSV. The export includes settled invoices and credit notes, but excludes pending card authorizations. Large exports are prepared asynchronously and remain downloadable for 24 hours.

title: Account data deletion
text: A verified account owner can request deletion from Privacy Settings. The service schedules user content for deletion within 30 days, except records retained for fraud prevention, legal obligations, or active billing disputes. The confirmation receipt names the request date and applicable exceptions.

title: Enterprise audit log retention
text: Enterprise audit events remain searchable for 365 days. Each event records actor, action, target, timestamp, and request identifier. Administrators can stream events to their own archive for longer retention. Editing or deleting an audit event through the product is not supported.

title: Workspace chat retention
text: A workspace owner may set chat retention to 30, 60, or 90 days. The default is 90 days. Shortening the period schedules older conversations for deletion, while legal holds suspend deletion for the affected records. Audit-event retention is configured separately.

title: European data residency
text: Eligible enterprise workspaces can select the European Union region when the workspace is created. Primary customer content and new backups remain in the selected region. Some account, abuse-prevention, and billing metadata is processed globally as described in the data map. An existing workspace cannot switch regions in place.

title: Package marked delivered but missing
text: When tracking says delivered but the package is missing, check the delivery photo and nearby safe locations, then wait 48 hours because carriers sometimes scan early. If the package remains missing after 48 hours, open a carrier investigation with the order number and delivery address.

title: Report a damaged shipment
text: Report a damaged shipment within seven days of delivery. Keep the packaging and attach photographs of the item, outer box, and shipping label. Support may issue a replacement after reviewing the evidence. Do not return a damaged battery until the safety team supplies instructions.

title: Enterprise support response targets
text: Priority 1 incidents target an initial human response within 30 minutes at all hours. Priority 2 incidents target four business hours. These are response targets, not resolution promises. The customer must provide impact, affected users, recent changes, and a reachable incident contact.

title: Plan changes and proration
text: An upgrade takes effect immediately and creates a prorated charge for the remainder of the billing period. A downgrade takes effect at the next renewal date and does not create a cash credit. Removing seats changes the next invoice after the seat commitment ends.

title: Password reset links
text: A password reset link expires 15 minutes after issuance and can be used once. Requesting another link invalidates earlier links. If the message does not arrive, check the spam folder and verify that the account email was entered exactly.

title: Mobile offline mode
text: Offline mode keeps previously opened documents available on one mobile device for up to seven days. Edits queue locally and synchronize after connectivity returns. Signing out removes offline copies. Conflict resolution preserves both versions and asks the user to choose.

title: Notification preferences
text: Users control email and push notifications from Personal Settings. Security alerts and billing receipts cannot be disabled. Project updates can be batched daily, sent immediately, or muted. Workspace owners cannot silently change a member's personal notification preference.

title: Rollback a model deployment
text: Each production model release points to an immutable model, prompt, tokenizer, and policy bundle. To roll back, move the deployment alias to the last known good bundle, drain incompatible in-flight work, and verify a fixed smoke suite. Never overwrite the failed bundle because incident replay needs it.

title: Backup and restore
text: Encrypted backups run every six hours and are retained for 35 days. A quarterly restore drill writes into an isolated account and verifies record counts, referential integrity, and deletion tombstones. A successful backup job is not evidence that restoration works.
